name: Armand J. Piron
abstract: Armand John "A.J." Piron (August 16, 1888 – February 17, 1943) was an American jazz violinist who led dance bands during the 1910s thru the 1930s.

works_created.t: I Wish I Could Shimmy Like My Sister Kate

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Bauduc
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: ...how did Robichaud's band compare with Piron's band? You know, Armand J. Piron? INTERVIEWEE: Well, Piron, well, who was the left-hand fiddle player?

about_quotes.speaker: Hal Smith
about_quotes.evidence: plays it the way it was meant to be played, and not with modern chords and ride cymbal and all of that, it’s very authentic... Armand Piron
about_quotes.doc_title: Hal Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Natty Dominique
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah, they had Armand Piron, Peter Bocard, Nanya Perez, George Moray, Big Gasper, George Field, Shalini. He had Cartel on drums.

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: You wouldn’t expect Chris Kelly to play them funky lowdown blues where Piron played... Each level of society had their music. It was a well calculated thing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Danny Barker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
